metadata.title: Antifragile: Things That Gain from Disorder — Book Review

metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. The book shifts from predicting events to reacting well when they inevitably arrive. Its methodological core is that fragility stays measurable even when probability is not. Not all acceptance of downside is noble; some exposure is simply reckless harm. Examples like medicated children and dulled pilots show fragility created by intervention.

artifactLines: The book shifts from predicting events to reacting well when they inevitably arrive.
Its methodological core is that fragility stays measurable even when probability is not.
Not all acceptance of downside is noble; some exposure is simply reckless harm.
Examples like medicated children and dulled pilots show fragility created by intervention.

beats.narration_text: The distinction that fragility is measurable even when probability is not, and the shift from predictive to reactive thinking, is what I want to actually operate. Show me: (1) how you would measure the fragility of a system without estimating any probabilities, (2) why reactive thinking beats prediction when the risk is unknowable, and (3) one everyday decision, like the medicated children or dulled pilots examples, reframed as reducing fragility instead of forecasting.
